page: Google Cloud Platform c2-standard-16 is a compute-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs across 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The CPU configuration includes a 24 MB L3 cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 KB L1d cache. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include bundled local storage or GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks position this instance firmly in the average tier for most compute and memory operations. The Intel Xeon processor delivers a single-core stress-ng score of 1574.85 and a multi-core score of 16636.04. In cryptographic and vector-heavy workloads, the CPU utilizes AVX-512 extensions, yielding a Passmark CPU extended instructions score of 14785.37 and an encryption score of 7646.43. Memory performance is a notable strength, with a Passmark memory score of 2768.04 placing it in the top 25% of tested systems, supported by a cached read-only small block memory bandwidth of 926.61 GB/sec.

For specialized workloads, the c2-standard-16 demonstrates steady throughput. It achieves a Redis SET performance of 15.61 million operations per second and a static web serving throughput of 11.93 GB/sec. Software compilation via Clang scores 14171.0 on Geekbench, while single-threaded gzip compression reaches 26.75 GB/sec. Large-language model inference speeds are moderate, processing 744.96 tokens per second for a small 135M model prompt, but dropping to a weaker 23.87 tokens per second for a medium 7B model prompt.

Given its dedicated compute resources and strong memory performance, the c2-standard-16 is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. It is well-suited for high-performance web serving, software compilation, database operations, and moderate-scale data processing tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2-standard-16 is a compute-optimized server featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 3.1 GHz. It lacks bundled local storage and GPU accelerators. The hardware configuration delivers solid, balanced performance, highlighted by a Passmark memory score in the top 25% and a cached read-only memory bandwidth of 926.61 GB/sec. Benchmark results show average multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 16636.04 and a Redis SET throughput of 15.61 million operations per second. While it handles small-scale LLM inference adequately, performance drops on larger models. This instance is best suited for compute-intensive workloads, software compilation, static web serving, and database operations where dedicated CPU resources and strong memory performance are required without the need for local NVMe storage.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2-standard-16 is a compute-optimized server with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and strong memory performance, ideal for compilation and database workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2-standard-16 is a compute-optimized server with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and no local storage.
tagline: A dedicated compute-optimized instance featuring high-frequency Intel Xeon processors and strong memory performance for demanding CPU-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by a 3.1 GHz Intel Xeon CPU
64.0 GB of system memory delivering strong memory bandwidth performance
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators included
Optimized for software compilation, database operations, and static web serving
Supports AVX-512 vector and cryptographic instruction sets
